description_short: Security Service FCU is a San Antonio-based federal credit union founded 1956 with $13B+ in assets. NCUA insured. 70+ branches in TX, CO, UT. BBB A+ accredited. Originally serving military/federal employees.
description_long:
Security Service Federal Credit Union (SSFCU) is a federally chartered credit union headquartered in San Antonio, Texas, founded in 1956. Originally established to serve military and federal employees, SSFCU has expanded to serve a broader membership base and manages over $13 billion in assets. The credit union operates more than 70 branches across Texas, Colorado, and Utah, with full-service banking including checking and savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, credit cards, and investment services.

As an NCUA-insured federal credit union, SSFCU offers depositor protection up to $250,000 per account and typically provides more competitive loan rates than for-profit banks and online lenders. The credit union structure means earnings are returned to members through better rates and lower fees rather than shareholder profits. SSFCU holds BBB A+ accreditation.

With a 3.5-star Google rating from 1,765 reviews, SSFCU's customer satisfaction is moderate for a large financial institution. Common credit union advantages include lower personal loan APRs, fewer fees, and community-focused service. Membership eligibility may require living in certain areas or affiliation with qualifying organizations.

As a consumer lender, this company competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
diagnosis: SSFCU is a large, well-established credit union with $13B+ in assets, NCUA insurance, and BBB A+ accreditation. Credit union personal loans typically offer APRs of 7-18% — dramatically lower than online subprime lenders. The 3.5 Google rating suggests room for improvement in customer service. Best for consumers eligible for membership who want competitive banking and lending rates.
pros: Federally insured credit union with NCUA backing provides deposit safety up to $250,000
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits benefit members through lower rates and fees
Multiple branches and ATM network across San Antonio and South Texas region
Offers full banking services including checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards
Military-friendly with services for active duty, veterans, and Department of Defense employees
cons: Membership eligibility may be restricted to specific geographic areas or employer groups
Branch network limited to San Antonio and surrounding South Texas communities
Technology and mobile app capabilities may lag behind large national banks or fintech companies
Product selection may be narrower than large commercial banks
services: Personal loans and lines of credit
Mobile banking and online account management
Military and DOD employee banking services
